items.title: Rust-proof your code with our new Testing Handbook chapter
items.summary: We’ve added a new chapter to our Testing Handbook: a comprehensive guide to security testing Rust programs. This chapter covers the tools and techniques we use at Trail of Bits to validate the security of Rust programs and systems. fn main() {(|f:&dyn Fn(u128)->Box< dyn Iterator<

items.title: GPT-5.5-Cyber built a zlib fuzzing lab in a day
items.summary: We’re running Patch the Planet, an ongoing collaboration with OpenAI that pairs Trail of Bits engineers directly with more than 30 open-source projects. Its goal is to front-run a serious problem facing open-source maintainers: highly capable models like GPT-5.5-Cyber will soon c

items.title: Bitcoin under $63,000 after new U.S. strike on Iran. Trump's China comment adds to uncertainty
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions and renewed fears of U.S.-China frictions are weighing on risk assets, including bitcoin.

items.title: Ethics in crypto market structure ‘really not our concern,’ says Blockchain Association CEO
items.summary: A former CFTC commissioner urged lawmakers not to “kill all the hard work that we put in the rest of the bill” by making ethics a make-or-break issue on crypto market structure.

items.title: Mutation testing comes to DAML
items.summary: In April we released Mewt, our open-source mutation-testing engine that finds the gaps in your test suite. Today we’re expanding it with support for DAML, the language Canton Network applications are written in. Mewt now reads DAML, generates several classes of mutants (including

items.title: Airbnb CEO says X account was hacked, attacker posted AI-slop on tokenization
items.summary: Brian Chesky regained control of his account and told any new crypto followers he would be a "disappointing follow".

items.title: Ether falls twice as hard as bitcoin and HYPE drops 10% as the chip trade unwinds
items.summary: Japan's Nikkei had its worst day since March. Ether is still the only major holding a gain on the week, barely.

items.title: This $28 million ether market bet aims to profit from pure market chaos
items.summary: The mega trade aims to profit from a potential surge in ether price turbulence.

items.title: Bitcoin Ordinals leader proposes a way to bypass data restriction efforts
items.summary: While BIP 110 wants to restrict data through a consensus change and has almost no miner support, a new DOG Mode client wants the opposite and requires no vote at all.

items.title: MacOS malware hijacks Telegram sessions, targets crypto wallets: SlowMist
items.summary: A macOS malware steals credentials to hijack Telegram sessions, decrypt cryptocurrency wallets or trick users into entering their wallet recovery phrases through fake applications.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs add $368M in three-day buying streak
items.summary: US spot Bitcoin ETFs attracted $79.2 million on Thursday, lifting their three-day inflow total to about $368 million as Bitcoin attempted a price recovery.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Ordinals advocate Leonidas proposes new Bitcoin client: ‘$DOG Mode’
items.summary: “Over time the economic incentives will drive $DOG Mode’s adoption and force Bitcoin Core to stop gatekeeping and allow these completely valid transactions,” said Leonidas.

items.title: Crypto exchange Bybit launches in Indonesia after NOBI acquisition
items.summary: The launch gives Bybit a locally operated platform in one of Asia’s largest crypto markets, home to more than 21 million crypto exchange users.

items.title: Trump Media sells Wall Street low-latency access to Trump posts
items.summary: The paid Truth API is aimed at high-frequency trading firms seeking “the fastest” access to market-moving Truth Social posts, including those from US President Donald Trump.

items.title: Balaji seeks Malaysia deal, threatens exit after Network School probe
items.summary: “If we are not welcome, there are many other countries that would welcome us,” said Network School founder Balaji Srinivasan in a message to the Malaysian prime minister.

items.title: Bitcoin liquidity clusters determine BTC’s price direction as futures flow fuels price
items.summary: Bitcoin futures traders are propping up the current market move, while liquidation heatmap data provides hints on where BTC price may go next.

items.title: $1.9 trillion asset manager T. Rowe Price bets on active management with first multi-token crypto ETF
items.summary: T. Rowe Price launched what it says is the industry's first actively managed multi-token spot crypto ETF, offering diversified exposure to digital assets.

items.title: Citadel Securities invests $400 million in Crypto.com, valuing exchange at $20 billion
items.summary: The exchange's first institutional funding round values it at $20 billion and will fund expansion into tokenized securities and derivatives.

items.title: Stripe’s $53 billion PayPal bid is a high-stakes play to own the future of digital payments
items.summary: If Stripe acquires PayPal, the real prize could be consumer wallets, stablecoin issuance or the infrastructure powering the next generation of digital payments.
